LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS DENNIS GOZA, INDIVIDUALLY AND AS TRUSTEE, MARGARET GOZA, AS TRUSTEE'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. ARGUED AND THE COURT TOOK THE MATTER UNDER SUBMISSION. JUDGE: LORETTA M. GIORGI, REPORTER: KARLA ELLIS-DAVIS, CSR #12998 --- AFTER HEARING, THE COURT RULED AS FOLLOWS: THE COURT DENIES DEFENDANTS' MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. PLAINTIFF HAS PRESENTED A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT AS TO WHETHER PLAINTIFF'S INJURIES WERE CAUSED BY SOMETHING OTHER THAN THE FIRE, NAMELY THE BARRICADE ON THE DOOR TO THE WAREHOUSE WHERE THE FIRE OCCURRED. MOREOVER, IF THERE WAS A BARRICADE, PLAINTIFF HAS RAISED A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT AS TO WHETHER THE BARRICADE WAS IN VIOLATION OF THE SAN FRANCISCO BUILDING CODE. THESE FACTUAL ISSUES MUST BE DETERMINED IN ORDER TO RESOLVE THE LEGAL ISSUE OF WHETHER THE FIREFIGHTER'S RULE BARS PLAINTIFF'S RECOVERY OR IF CC 1714.9 APPLIES TO RE-IMPOSE A DUTY OF CARE OTHERWISE ABROGATED BY THE FIREFIGHTER'S RULE. PARTIES NOTIFIED OF THE COURT'S RULING BY TELEPHONE. PLAINTIFF IS DIRECTED TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER.

Off Calendar Aug-16-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS DENNIS GOZA, INDIVIDUALLY AND AS TRUSTEE, MARGARET GOZA, AS TRUSTEE'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT.
Appearances: Thomas J. Brandi of The Brandi Law Firm (989.1800) for plaintiff and responding party; Shiva Tavakolu and Spiros Fousekis of the Narayan Law Firm (650.403.0150) for defendants and moving parties.
Argued and the court took the matter under submission. (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi, Reporter: Karla Ellis-Davis, CSR #12998
---
After hearing, the court ruled as follows: The Court denies Defendants' Motion for Summary Judgment. Plaintiff has presented a triable issue of material fact as to whether Plaintiff's injuries were caused by something other than the fire, namely the barricade on the door to the warehouse where the fire occurred. Moreover, if there was a barricade, Plaintiff has raised a triable issue of material fact as to whether the barricade was in violation of the San Francisco Building Code. These factual issues must be determined in order to resolve the legal issue of whether the Firefighter's Rule bars Plaintiff's recovery or if CC 1714.9 applies to re-impose a duty of care otherwise abrogated by the Firefighter's Rule. Parties notified of the court's ruling by telephone. Plaintiff is directed to submit a proposed form of order.
